And so that has nothing to do with technology. And so to do things in such messy systems, you still need people. You still need people to navigate all these changes. It's not about the capability of technology. It's more about how the system is structured. And that's why I still feel there will be new jobs that maybe the you know there's a lot of new projects to be done.
turns.zh: 没错。是的。所以老实说，比 AI 更严重的是，政府运行着大量遗留软件栈，因为很多遗留企业公司签了长达数十年或多年的合同，很难摆脱。他们通过大幅折扣来销售。比如，如果你使用某个特定操作系统，十年内不允许更换。你必须使用同一套软件。你雇佣的所有人只知道使用那个工具。所以实际改变和实施新事物需要时间。更不用说 AI 了。就算你只想把所有人从 Windows 机器换成 Mac 机器，祝你好运。那将花费大量时间。这就是系统的现状。而这与技术无关。所以在如此混乱的系统中做事，你仍然需要人。你仍然需要人来驾驭所有这些变化。这不是技术能力的问题，而是系统结构的问题。这就是为什么我仍然认为会有新工作，也许你知道，有很多新项目要做。

transcript.secZh: AI 时代重新定义工作与热情

turns.en: Maybe some good leader actually wants to change the system and is willing to be patient about it. Like, over a 5 to 10 year horizon, if you take 10 years to actually run majority of the government processes on AIs, it may seem slow to you today, but in the grand scheme of things, it's actually good for the country. And that's still going to need a lot of nice engineers to go work on these projects, so they're not going to lose all their jobs. There's going to be some displacement, there's going to be some new projects, there's going to be new priorities, but it'll keep going. The system will keep going because that's just how historically things have been.
turns.zh: 也许有些好的领导者真的想改变这个系统，并且愿意对此保持耐心。比如，在 5 到 10 年的时间跨度内，如果你花 10 年时间让 AI 真正运行大部分政府流程，今天看来可能很慢，但从大局来看，这对国家实际上是有好处的。而且这仍然需要很多优秀的工程师去从事这些项目，所以他们不会失去所有工作。会有一些岗位被取代，也会有一些新项目、新优先事项，但系统会继续运转。历史上事情就是这样发展的。

turns.en: When you think about the future of AI and you think of AGI in particular, you think about something that could potentially make better versions of itself.
turns.zh: 当你思考 AI 的未来，特别是 AGI 时，你会想到一种可能制造出更好版本自身的东西。

turns.en: Self-replicating.
turns.zh: 自我复制。

turns.en: Yeah, and then how far does it go?
turns.zh: 是的，然后它能走多远？

turns.en: Yeah, so that is the ultimate form. I think some people in Silicon Valley have started calling that ASI. So when you see the word ASI being thrown around, people kind of think of ASI as an AGI that can recursively self-improve itself. So that's going to be no limits to how smart it can get.
turns.zh: 是的，这就是终极形态。我认为硅谷的一些人已经开始称之为 ASI。所以当你看到 ASI 这个词被提及时，人们大致认为 ASI 是一种能够递归自我改进的 AGI。所以它的智能程度将没有上限。

turns.en: Right.
turns.zh: 对。

turns.en: And I used to think that ASI is bottlenecked by power because you need a ton of compute for this model to keep on training itself and running its own rollouts and collecting data and then going and updating itself. But you could imagine that once the algorithm is correct, the ASI could be tasked with just making itself more efficient to where improvement doesn't just mean capability improvement. Improvement could also mean power efficiency, and that way the recursive safe ASI that is improving itself also makes itself more compact and more efficient, and it can run on less compute. So that would be the ultimate project in AI. Think of it as almost the last project in AI is basically cracking recursive self-improvement. Once you crack that, you don't have anything else to work on.
turns.zh: 我以前认为 ASI 受限于能源，因为你需要大量算力让这个模型不断训练自己、运行自己的生成、收集数据，然后更新自己。但你可以想象，一旦算法正确，ASI 可以被赋予任务，只是让自己更高效，这样改进就不仅仅意味着能力提升。改进也可能意味着能效提升，这样递归安全的 ASI 在自我改进的同时也变得更紧凑、更高效，并且可以在更少的算力上运行。所以这将是 AI 的终极项目。几乎可以认为，AI 的最后一个项目就是破解递归自我改进。一旦你破解了它，就没有其他事情可做了。

turns.en: In practice, I think what's going to happen is, because information is so muddled and fragmented and living in disjoint systems, just the way we have constructed our messy real world, it's going to be hard to point even a recursively self-improving AI at some metric and say 'go improve this' or 'go reduce inflation by 5%.' That would be awesome if you can task an AI to do that. If that's the job of the government to just reduce inflation, have a deflationary effect on society and make goods and services a lot more abundant and efficient, it's going to have to deal with a lot of messy legacy systems. If the task is to go improve healthcare, good luck. Like, who's going to deal with all the compliance of actually implementing these changes inside hospitals? Most hospitals are still using legacy software because that software provider has lobbied the government in a way where only they are allowed to do that.
turns.zh: 在实践中，我认为会发生的是，由于信息如此混乱、碎片化，并且存在于不连贯的系统中，就像我们构建的混乱现实世界一样，即使是一个递归自我改进的 AI，也很难指向某个指标说“去改进这个”或“把通货膨胀降低 5%”。如果你能委托 AI 做到这一点，那将非常棒。如果政府的任务就是降低通货膨胀，对社会产生通缩效应，并使商品和服务更加丰富和高效，那么它将不得不处理许多混乱的遗留系统。如果任务是改进医疗保健，那祝你好运。比如，谁来处理医院内部实际实施这些变革的所有合规问题？大多数医院仍然使用遗留软件，因为该软件提供商游说政府，使得只有他们被允许这样做。

turns.en: God, what a stupid bottleneck.
turns.zh: 天哪，真是个愚蠢的瓶颈。

turns.en: Exactly. So, a lot of the bottlenecks in actually having AIs just take over and massively improve human society and our hospitals, our legal systems, our government systems where most of the payroll is going into, is just bottlenecked by a lot of compliance and regulation. And so that's why I feel we human beings are still necessary to effect the change because these laws and regulations were built for us.
turns.zh: 没错。所以，实际上让 AI 接管并大规模改善人类社会、我们的医院、法律体系、政府体系（大部分工资都投入其中）的许多瓶颈，就是被大量的合规和监管所限制。这就是为什么我觉得人类仍然需要去推动变革，因为这些法律法规是为我们而制定的。

turns.en: And it also seems like we have to demand that those systems be usurped.
turns.zh: 而且似乎我们也必须要求这些系统被取代。

turns.en: Sure. 100%. And we need the help of AIs to rewrite all these laws.
turns.zh: 当然。百分之百。而且我们需要 AI 的帮助来重写所有这些法律。

turns.en: It's going to be humanly impossible to go and change one specific line here and there.
turns.zh: 人类不可能去这里那里修改某一行具体条文。

turns.en: Right. And then you're going to have a bunch of these software companies that are lobbying to try to stop that from happening. And yeah, that's why this messiness and this need for getting all people on the same page and actually steering the society in a positive way. Our jobs will probably be more steered towards that problem solving at a different level of abstraction. Maybe more need for EQ, more need for actually understanding differences of opinion and still like a leadership quality, ability to understand people and ability to convince people. These are the skills that will be even more important in a world where actual work can be done by AIs but affecting the change in our society in our country still needs human beings because the systems are messy.
turns.zh: 对。然后你会有一堆软件公司游说试图阻止这种情况发生。是的，这就是为什么这种混乱以及让所有人达成共识并真正以积极方式引导社会的需求。我们的工作可能会更多地转向在更高抽象层次上解决这些问题。也许更需要情商，更需要真正理解意见分歧，以及领导力、理解他人和说服他人的能力。这些技能在一个实际工作可以由 AI 完成的世界里将变得更加重要，但影响我们社会、我们国家的变革仍然需要人类，因为系统是混乱的。

turns.en: It's a weird world we're in right now.
turns.zh: 我们现在所处的世界很奇怪。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: It's never been weirder. That said, there's a lot of things that can still go wrong when you give so much power to specific companies and they deploy all these bots and then anybody can use them in weird ways. You don't even know if you're talking to a real person anymore.
turns.zh: 从未如此奇怪。话虽如此，当你把如此多的权力交给特定公司，他们部署所有这些机器人，然后任何人都可以以奇怪的方式使用它们时，很多事情仍然可能出错。你甚至不知道你是否还在和真人说话。

turns.en: They're like people who just run AI responses and chat with like 500 people at once, and that's like a whole business. And so, I think it's going to take a lot of adjustment.
turns.zh: 他们就像那些运行 AI 回复并同时与 500 人聊天的人，这就像一门完整的生意。所以，我认为这需要大量的调整。

turns.en: Well, another piece of adjustment that a lot of people are coming to grips with is that this is a new part of our conversation. And that in 2020, like when I first moved here, AI was never discussed.
turns.zh: 嗯，很多人正在适应的另一件事是，这是我们对话的一个新部分。在 2020 年，比如我刚搬到这里时，AI 从未被讨论过。

turns.en: It was not a thing.
turns.zh: 它根本不算个事。

turns.en: Yeah. I mean, we knew about it, we knew about AI, but it wasn't a huge part of the cultural discussion of what the future holds for us.
turns.zh: 是的。我的意思是，我们知道它，我们知道 AI，但它并不是关于未来对我们意味着什么的文化讨论的主要部分。

turns.en: And now it is. Now it is central.
turns.zh: 而现在它是了。现在它是核心。

turns.en: And in that short amount of time, in just six years, it really makes you wonder because we know how technology progresses exponentially, like what it's going to look like 6 years from now.
turns.zh: 在这么短的时间内，仅仅六年，这真的让你思考，因为我们知道技术是如何指数级进步的，比如六年后的样子。

turns.en: Yeah. My prediction is that the 2028 election debates are going to be largely about AI.
turns.zh: 是的。我的预测是，2028 年的选举辩论将主要围绕 AI 展开。

turns.en: Wow.
turns.zh: 哇。

turns.en: Yeah. AI energy crisis, the power people are going to care about all these things. Because AI is no longer a thing that is new. It's part of all our lives. Everyone's using some form of AI in some ways, and it's not as dangerous as people thought. It's an amazing tool for doing work and asking questions and learning things and all these things when used correctly.
turns.zh: 是的。AI 能源危机，人们会关心所有这些事情。因为 AI 不再是一个新事物。它已成为我们生活的一部分。每个人都在以某种方式使用某种形式的 AI，而且它并不像人们想象的那么危险。当正确使用时，它是一个用于工作、提问、学习等所有事情的惊人工具。

turns.en: Can also be used incorrectly.
turns.zh: 也可能被错误使用。

turns.en: Like everything.
turns.zh: 就像所有事物一样。

turns.en: So it's far more powerful that incorrect usage can cause serious damage. For example, kids who are using AIs for companionship — crazy things are happening there. It's as dangerous as, or probably more dangerous than, social media. And it's also scary that social media companies want to build more of these companionship apps because they know their only job was to get you engaged more, and that's the only way to sell more ads and make more money. Clearly, companionship is a way to get you engaged more. And so that's dangerous. If ads start being part of AI chats, because if that ends up working, then all these chatbots are just going to be sycophants that tell you stuff you want to hear. It's also an indistinguishable facsimile of a real person. They communicate like a real person, right? So you really think you have a relationship with this, and it truly screws with your mind. It's hard to decouple, and it takes a lot of time to recover if you want to unplug. So the business model incentives are not well aligned to humanity.
turns.zh: 所以它更强大，错误使用会造成严重伤害。例如，孩子们把 AI 当作陪伴——那里正在发生疯狂的事情。它和社交媒体一样危险，甚至更危险。而且可怕的是，社交媒体公司想开发更多这类陪伴应用，因为他们知道自己的唯一任务就是让你更投入，这是卖更多广告、赚更多钱的唯一途径。显然，陪伴是让你更投入的一种方式。所以这很危险。如果广告开始成为 AI 聊天的一部分，因为如果这奏效了，那么所有这些聊天机器人就会变成只会说你想听的话的马屁精。它还是真人无法区分的仿制品。它们像真人一样交流，对吧？所以你真的以为你和它有关系，这真的会搞乱你的大脑。很难脱身，如果你想断开连接，需要很长时间才能恢复。所以商业模式激励与人类福祉并不一致。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: Did you see that AI companion they developed that was at the Consumer Electronic Show in Vegas this year?
turns.zh: 你看到今年拉斯维加斯消费电子展上他们开发的那个 AI 伴侣了吗？

turns.en: Which one?
turns.zh: 哪一个？

turns.en: It's like a hot Asian lady.
turns.zh: 就像一个火辣的亚洲女士。

turns.en: I see. Yeah. These are the weird kind of projects that are going on.
turns.zh: 我明白了。是的。这些就是正在进行的奇怪项目。

turns.en: Yeah. It's a hot Asian lady that talks to you. And right now it's just a crude sort of robot, but you could see where it's going.
turns.zh: 是的。是一个跟你说话的火辣亚洲女士。现在它只是一个粗糙的机器人，但你可以看到它的发展方向。

turns.en: You can see where it's going. Ex Machina.
turns.zh: 你可以看到它的发展方向。《机械姬》。

turns.en: Yeah. Right there.
turns.zh: 是的。就是那样。

turns.en: Yeah. That movie was amazing. Quite ahead of its time.
turns.zh: 是的。那部电影太棒了。相当超前。

turns.en: Really? It's one of my top 10 favorite movies of all time. It's underrated actually, because people reviews online say it's not as good, but I liked it. I loved it. I thought it was fantastic. I like it better than Her.
turns.zh: 真的吗？它是我有史以来最喜欢的十大电影之一。实际上它被低估了，因为网上评论说它没那么好，但我喜欢它。我爱它。我觉得它太棒了。我更喜欢它胜过《她》。

turns.en: Yeah, Her I lost after a while. I shut it off. It lost my attention. I'm sure it's good. It was the wrong time for me to watch it. But Ex Machina, I've seen it like five times. I love that movie. It's just so incredible and so bleak and in the relationship that he has with the hot one. You believe it. You're like, I'd be right there with him. You know, it's too confusing to our system to have something that looks exactly like the thing that you desire that is actually interested in you. It just happens to be all your data about stuff. Knows too much about you. Knows how to pull your strings.
turns.zh: 是的，《她》我看了一会儿就看不下去了。我关掉了。它失去了我的注意力。我相信它很好，只是我看的时机不对。但《机械姬》，我看了大概五遍。我爱那部电影。它太不可思议了，太凄凉了，以及他和那个火辣女郎的关系。你相信它。你会想，我会和他一样。你知道，有一个看起来完全像你渴望的东西，而且实际上对你感兴趣，这对我们的系统来说太混乱了。它恰好拥有你所有的数据。它太了解你了。知道如何操控你。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: But listen, man, very fascinating discussion. I'm glad we did it. Thank you very much.
turns.zh: 但是听着，伙计，非常精彩的讨论。我很高兴我们进行了这次对话。非常感谢。

turns.en: Thank you so much.
turns.zh: 非常感谢。

turns.en: And thanks for having an awesome platform. Perplexity has been great. We really love using it here at the show. It's made the show more interesting. It's cool.
turns.zh: 感谢你拥有一个很棒的平台。Perplexity 一直很棒。我们真的很喜欢在节目中使用它。它让节目更有趣。很酷。

turns.en: Thank you. It's very fulfilling because we want the app to be used by curious people like that. We want to lift the ceiling of what our population can be, you know? Not everyone is fully curious all the time, but we're all born with it. So, at some point in time, the system curbs it from us. So, there should be more apps that get us back to what we're naturally good at.
turns.zh: 谢谢。这很有成就感，因为我们希望这个应用被像你这样好奇的人使用。我们想提升我们人群可能达到的上限，你知道吗？不是每个人都一直充满好奇，但我们生来就有好奇心。所以，在某个时候，系统抑制了我们的好奇心。因此，应该有更多应用让我们回到我们天生擅长的状态。

turns.en: Yeah, it's a fascinating tool for technology or for curiosity rather, because to be able and it's seamless the way we use it on the show, because there's always a question. Yeah, there's always it comes up so often like throw it in Perplexity. Let's find out what's up. It's always been great for us. So, thank you.
turns.zh: 是的，这是一个迷人的工具，用于技术或者更确切地说是好奇心，因为我们在节目中无缝地使用它，因为总是有问题。是的，总是有疑问，经常这样，比如扔进 Perplexity。让我们查查怎么回事。它对我们一直很好。所以，谢谢你。

turns.en: Thank you so much.
turns.zh: 非常感谢。

turns.en: All right. My pleasure. Bye everybody.
turns.zh: 好的。我的荣幸。大家再见。
